org.arakhne.neteditor.figlayout.sugiyama

Class GanswerSugiyamaFigureLayout

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arakhne.neteditor.figlayout.AbstractFigureLayout
      • org.arakhne.neteditor.figlayout.AbstractDirectionBasedFigureLayout
        • org.arakhne.neteditor.figlayout.sugiyama.GanswerSugiyamaFigureLayout
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ViewComponentConstants, FigureLayout, FigureLayoutConstants

    public class GanswerSugiyamaFigureLayout
extends AbstractDirectionBasedFigureLayout
    This class represents the Ganswer-Sugiyama Layout Algorithm. The approach was first described by Gansner et al. in A Technique for Drawing Directed Graphs (1993). The Algorithm works only for non cyclic Graphs. This implementation contains three steps:
    1. Ranking: The Nodes divided into different layers. For example: The first layer contains all nodes having no incoming edges.
    2. Ordering: This Phase tries to reduce the number of crossings. There are different approaches possible: Here I use the Barycenter-Method. There are other methods thinkable.
    3. Position: Place all the Node (and Edges) on a right place in the graph.

    That means a long edge (over more then one layer) should be straight. The current solution is not very good and should be improved.

    This implementation was adapted to cyclic graphs by Stéphane GALLAND.

    Version:
    NetEditor Figure Layout $Revision$ 2013-10-23 21:03:08
    Author:
    Marc BAUMGARTNER, Stéphane GALLAND
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-figlayout

    • Method Detail

      • getPreferredInterLayerSpace

        public float getPreferredInterLayerSpace()
        Replies the preferred size to allocate to the space between the layers.
        Returns:
        the preferred size to allocate to the space between the layers.

      • setPreferredInterLayerSpace

        public void setPreferredInterLayerSpace(float size)
        Set the preferred size to allocate to the space between the layers.
        Parameters:
        size - is the preferred size to allocate to the space between the layers.

      • layoutFigures

        public Undoable layoutFigures(Collection<? extends Figure> figures)
        Run the laying-out algorithm for the given figures.
        Parameters:
        figures - are the figures to laying out.
        Returns:
        the undoable edit that permits to revert the laying out; or null if the layout does not supports the undo/redo feature or it has nothing to undo.
